summary: Emergency physicians can accurately and reliably assess acute vertigo through systematic evaluation methods including detailed history-taking, targeted physical examination, and prompt identification of critical red flags indicating potential central nervous system involvement—ensuring timely interventions even amid inherent diagnostic complexity.
